message:
fix(storage): make dedup-check aware of active embedding jobs to prevent double-enqueue

pipeline.rs: the dedup query now joins against embedding_jobs to detect
whether a pending or processing job already exists for the document +
embedder pair.  If an active job is found, the document is classified as
Unchanged regardless of its embedding_status row, preventing redundant
re-enqueue when the same document is ingested while a job is still
in-flight.

This fixes a race condition where rapidly re-ingesting the same document
(e.g., via watch-mode or batch retries) could enqueue duplicate embedding
jobs, wasting compute and potentially causing vector-store write
conflicts.

Includes a new unit test
`ingest_unchanged_document_with_pending_job_is_not_reenqueued` that
exercises the exact scenario.  Also corrects the batch_ingest summary
assertions to reflect the improved dedup accuracy (previously the second
"doc-a" was counted as new work; now it is correctly classified as
unchanged).

schema.rs: extend schema definitions to support the new join path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
